Key Figures: Phil Bryant; Rachel Canter; Mississippi Department of Education
Policies/Mechanisms: Literacy-Based Promotion Act; reading coaches; structured literacy alignment; grade 3 promotion gate
Outcomes: Large grade 4 reading gains and national attention for literacy improvement

Core Focus: Evidence-based reading instruction, structured literacy, early screening
Key Figures: Louisa Moats; Tim Shanahan; state literacy offices
Policies/Mechanisms: Explicit phonics; decoding instruction; screening and intervention systems; teacher training
Outcomes: Adopted widely across states but not a single evaluable program

Policies/Mechanisms: Shared standards; aligned assessments; implementation guidance; curriculum alignment
Outcomes: Mixed but measurable achievement effects with subgroup differences

Policies/Mechanisms: Last-dollar scholarships; leadership development; advising support
Outcomes: Reduced debt and work burden with stronger persistence and STEM pathways

Core Focus: Grade-level reading, early intervention, promotion gates
Policies/Mechanisms: Grade 3 reading benchmarks; retention provisions; mandated interventions; parent notification
Outcomes: Mixed evidence with some reading gains and important implementation tradeoffs

Initiative: No Child Left Behind annual testing/accountability
Core Focus: Annual state testing, subgroup accountability, sanctions
Key Figures: George W. Bush; U.S. Department of Education; Congress
Policies/Mechanisms: Annual reading and math testing; adequate yearly progress; subgroup reporting; school sanctions
Outcomes: Clearer gains in math than reading with stronger effects for lower-performing groups

Initiative: Chicago test-based promotion and accountability reforms
Key Figures: Chicago Public Schools; Brian Jacob; Lars Lefgren
Policies/Mechanisms: High-stakes exams; summer school; promotional gates; remediation assignment
Outcomes: High-stakes score gains plus strategic responses and uneven downstream benefits

Core Focus: School grades, accountability pressure, low-performing schools
Key Figures: Florida Department of Education; David Figlio; Cecilia Rouse
Policies/Mechanisms: A-F-style grading; sanctions; public labeling; voucher threat for repeated failure
Outcomes: Positive math-focused effects with evidence of stigma and test-specific responses

Core Focus: Career pathways, academy structure, labor market transitions
Policies/Mechanisms: Small learning communities; employer partnerships; career-themed curricula; work-based exposure
Outcomes: Sustained earnings gains without reducing high school completion or college participation

Core Focus: Technical pathways, employer partnerships, college credit
Policies/Mechanisms: Six-year diploma-to-degree model; dual enrollment; workplace learning; industry partnerships
Outcomes: Large internship and dual-enrollment gains with higher associate degree completion

Core Focus: Apprenticeship, paid work-based learning, career pathways
Policies/Mechanisms: Youth apprenticeships; employer matching; paid work experience; advising support
Outcomes: Strong pathway design and descriptive positive outcomes with limited causal evidence so far

Core Focus: Outdoor learning, nutrition education, hands-on instruction
Key Figures: TX Sprouts researchers; school garden educators
Policies/Mechanisms: Outdoor teaching gardens; cooking lessons; nutrition curriculum; hands-on science learning
Outcomes: Some positive elementary reading and science outcomes with heterogeneity by grade and measure

Initiative: School cellphone bans / phone-free school policies
Key Figures: Florida school districts; David Figlio; Umut Ozek
Policies/Mechanisms: Bell-to-bell phone bans; device restrictions; enforcement rules
Outcomes: Second-year test-score gains and lower absences alongside short-run suspension increases
